event: presented at emergency department with massive hemorrhage from tracheostomy tube
multiple traumas due to car accident
past history of discharge of food from foramen of tracheostomy tube after oral feeding
urgent surgery performed under general anesthesia
anesthetic agents administered via tracheostomy tube
tracheoesophageal fistula (TEF) in membranous part of trachea behind tracheostomy stoma
bleeding stopped due to pressure of tracheostomy cuff
division and ligature of innominate artery
separation of trachea from divided artery
tracheal defect repaired and reinforced with strap muscle
jejunostomy tube inserted due to TEF
right radial pulse weaker than left
large foramen in anterior wall of esophagus below upper esophageal sphincter
developed deep vein thrombosis in left leg
deep vein thrombosis progressed into inferior vena cava
heparin administered 1000 U/h via infusion pump
severe purulent discharge from tracheostomy tube
chest X-ray showed bronchiectasis and pneumonia in both lower pulmonary lobes
appropriate treatment started based on discharge culture
septic shock unresponsive to medical treatment
expired due to massive deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary sepsis
